title: Handle Alerts and Dialogs
description: Learn to handle JavaScript alerts, confirms, and prompts in Playwright
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Sets up a dialog handler before triggering
2. Accepts an alert dialog
3. Dismisses a confirm dialog
4. Handles a prompt with custom input
5. Verifies the results

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_handle_dialogs(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://practice.expandtesting.com/js-dialogs')
    
    # TODO: Handle alert dialog
    
    # TODO: Handle confirm dialog
    
    # TODO: Handle prompt dialog
    
    # TODO: Verify results
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_handle_dialogs(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://practice.expandtesting.com/js-dialogs')
    
    # Handle alert
    def handle_alert(dialog):
        assert dialog.type == 'alert'
        assert 'alert' in dialog.message
        dialog.accept()
    
    page.on('dialog', handle_alert)
    page.click('#js-alert')
    page.remove_listener('dialog', handle_alert)
    
    # Handle confirm - accept
    page.once('dialog', lambda dialog: dialog.accept())
    page.click('#js-confirm')
    expect(page.locator('#dialog-response')).to_contain_text('OK')
    
    # Handle prompt with input
    page.once('dialog', lambda dialog: dialog.accept('Playwright Test'))
    page.click('#js-prompt')
    expect(page.locator('#dialog-response')).to_contain_text('Playwright Test')

hints: Use page.on('dialog') to set up dialog handlers
Call dialog.accept() to accept the dialog
Call dialog.dismiss() to cancel the dialog
Use dialog.accept('text') to enter text in prompts

testCases.name: Confirm is handled
testCases.expectedBehavior: Confirm dialog is accepted and result verified
